data.title: PitchBook releases 2026 Outlook reports on global private markets trends highlighting key investments ahead.
data.description: In Seattle, PitchBook, a top private capital market intelligence provider, has published its 2026 Outlook report series, presenting data-driven insights into investment trends likely to influence private markets in the upcoming year. Comprising seven reports, the series draws on the knowledge of PitchBook's worldwide research team, covering areas such as venture capital, private equity, AI, emerging technology sectors, and established industries. Following a period of restricted liquidity and inconsistent deal flow, the reports aim to provide a comprehensive overview of market projections.

data.title: RGP CFO Survey: Gap between AI ambition and readiness widens in finance sector; hindrances include fragile data foundations and slow governance.
data.description: In Dallas, professional services firm RGP® (Nasdaq: RGP) has published a new report titled "The AI Foundational Divide: From Ambition to Readiness." This study delves into the growing disparity between high expectations for AI and the lack of operational preparedness needed to achieve tangible benefits. The report highlights the financial sector's rapid movement towards an AI-driven environment but also notes obstacles such as weak data infrastructure, outdated systems, and developing governance protocols.

data.title: Capcom's new IP PRAGMATA to launch on April 24, 2026, a sci-fi action-adventure game from Japan.
data.description: Capcom, a renowned gaming company based in Osaka, Japan, has announced the launch date for their latest project, PRAGMATA. This sci-fi action-adventure game is set to be released on April 24, 2026. PRAGMATA is a completely original intellectual property from Capcom, adding to their diverse gaming portfolio. Fans of the genre can look forward to immersing themselves in this new gaming experience set to be unveiled in just a few years.
Fed's Hammack: Job market break-even is greatly reduced.
FED'S HAMMACK SAYS FED'S DECISION THIS WEEK WAS COMPLICATED

data.title: MDE School gets $10,500 from GBC via Georgia SSO Tax Credit Program to aid low-income student scholarships.
data.description: MDE School recently disclosed that it has obtained a $10,500 donation from the Georgia Student Scholarship Organization (Georgia SSO) owing to Georgia Banking Company's (GBC) involvement in the state's tax credit initiative. The contribution will be allocated to need-based initiatives at the school.

data.title: CR Fitness Holdings LLC to open Crunch McKinney studio in Texas, a $12M facility spanning 40,000 sq ft.
data.description: On December 12, 2025, in Dallas, it was announced by CR Fitness Holdings LLC that Crunch McKinney, a new state-of-the-art fitness facility, will be opening soon. This facility is anticipated to open in 2026 and will span 40,000 square feet, with an investment of $12 million. CR Fitness Holdings LLC is a prominent franchisee of Crunch Fitness, known for its rapid growth and success in the industry.
Fed's Hammack: Right now, the Fed policy is right around neutral.
FED'S HAMMACK: WOULD PREFER FOR FED POLICY TO BE A LITTLE MORE RESTRICTIVE THAN CURRENT LEVEL

data.title: Perma-Pipe International Holdings, Inc. reports Q3 2025: Net sales $61.1 million (46.9% increase), gross profit $21.0 million.
data.description: Perma-Pipe International Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PPIH) reported its financial performance for the quarter ending October 31, 2025, from The Woodlands, Texas. The company disclosed that their net sales for this quarter reached $61.1 million, showcasing a significant surge of $19.5 million or 46.9% compared to the previous year’s $41.6 million during the same period. The boost was primarily attributed to increased sales volumes in the Middle East and North America. Additionally, the gross profit rose to $21.0 million, marking a $6.9 million increase from the prior year's $14.1 million.

data.title: LOBO integrates Shenzhen Xiangri's team into Jiangsu LOBO for PV energy; targets the energy storage power gen sector.
data.description: In Wuxi, China, on December 12, 2025, LOBO Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ: LOBO), a company specializing in electric mobility and smart energy solutions, shared that its subsidiary, Jiangsu LOBO Electric Vehicle Co. Ltd., has successfully brought on board the entire staff of Shenzhen Xiangri Technology Co., Ltd. This includes the research & development, sales, and business operation teams. This move has led to the formation of a Solar Division within Jiangsu LOBO, aimed at propelling the company into the photovoltaic energy-storage power generation market.
Fed's Hammack: Inflation is too high, stuck closer to 3% than 2%.

data.title: SYNEVIT® promotes healthy hair for men on International Men's Day. By age 35, two-thirds of American men face hair loss.
data.description: A skincare brand based in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, has introduced natural solutions to promote healthy hair in men as they age gracefully. With statistics showing that by the age of 35, two-thirds of American men may face hair-related concerns, the brand aims to provide products that support men in maintaining their physical edge. This initiative targets men who are seeking to address issues of hair loss and promote overall hair health. The brand's offerings are designed to cater to the specific needs of men looking to sustain their youthful appearance and confidence.

data.title: EMA recommends higher dose of Wegovy® for obesity, facilitating 20.7% weight loss on average, advancing in EU.
data.description: The European Medicines Agency (EMA) has approved the introduction of a new 7.2 mg dose of Wegovy® in the European Union (EU), marking a significant step forward in its availability in the region.
Fed's Hammack: Price stability over the long term will support job growth.

data.title: EdgeMode is conducting a portfolio review and boosting AI data center development in Spain.
data.description: EdgeMode recently revealed their plan to conduct a strategic review of their portfolio, while also focusing on advancing their AI data center development pipeline in Spain. This initiative aims to streamline their operations and optimize their investments to drive growth. The company is contemplating the potential divestment of certain assets to maximize value and generate more revenue. Additionally, they have stated that they will be increasing their investment in AI data center projects in Spain, with plans to invest approximately $50 million over the next year. This move aligns with their objective to capitalize on the growing demand for data centers in the region and strengthen their foothold in the market.

China strongly rejected the EU’s comments on its human rights situation. Beijing called on the EU to halt “megaphone diplomacy,” refrain from politicizing and instrumentalizing human rights issues, and stop interfering in the internal affairs of other countries.
